subject: [RFC PATCH v2 2/7] mm, swap: support zswap and zeroswap as vswap backends

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch adds support for zswap and zeroswap as virtual swap backends, allowing pages to be compressed or detected as zero-filled without pre-allocating physical swap slots. It builds on the existing swap-table infrastructure and introduces virtual swap entries that decouple PTE swap entries from physical backing. The patch only supports zswap and zero-page backends for now, with physical disk backing fallback and writeback of vswap-backed entries to be implemented in subsequent patches.

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Yosry Ahmed noted that a comment from this patch seems to belong in the previous patch, and the changes made here are undoing some of the work done in the last patch.

Reviewer Yosry Ahmed pointed out that the code should only execute in the !ret case, but this is not immediately clear from the current implementation. They suggested moving the check to an if (!ret) block and reordering the indentation to avoid confusion.

Reviewer Yosry Ahmed suggested avoiding THP swapin for vswap initially, noting that it's not specific to vswap and might be uncommon for zswap to have a large number of pages not in its cache.

Reviewer Yosry Ahmed questioned whether calling folio_release_vswap_backing() provides any advantage over zswap_entry_free(), noting that __vswap_release_backing() ultimately calls zswap_entry_free(). They suggested the zswap code should call zswap_entry_free() directly, as it appears to clean up vswap-specific state.

2026-06-23.patch_summary: This patch adds support for zswap and zeroswap as virtual swap backends, allowing pages to be compressed or detected as zero-filled without pre-allocating physical swap slots. It builds on top of the existing swap-table infrastructure and introduces virtual swap entries that decouple PTE swap entries from physical backing. The patch only supports zswap and zero-page backends for now, with physical disk backing fallback and writeback support to be added in subsequent patches.
